title: How Much Does It Cost to Hire an App Designer?
excerpt: App design costs vary greatly depending on complexity, platform, and designer experience. This post gives you a realistic breakdown of app design pricing so you can plan your budget, ask the right questions, and make a smart investment in your mobile product.
content:
The mobile app market has never been more competitive — and behind every successful app is a thoughtful, strategic design. But if you're planning to build an app for your business, one of your first questions is probably: how much is this going to cost?

The honest answer: it depends. But let's break it down so you can budget realistically.

App Design Cost Ranges

  • Basic App Design (1–5 screens): $1,000 – $5,000
  • Mid-Level App Design (10–20 screens): $5,000 – $20,000
  • Complex App Design (20+ screens, multiple user flows): $20,000 – $80,000+
These are design-only costs. If you also need development (turning designs into a working app), expect significantly higher total project costs.

What Drives App Design Pricing?

1. Number of Screens and User Flows Every screen in an app needs to be designed — from the onboarding flow to the dashboard to the settings page. More screens = more cost. 2. Platform(s) Are you building for iOS, Android, or both? Cross-platform design can sometimes be done efficiently, but native design for each platform adds time. 3. Complexity of UX Apps with complex interactions — animations, gesture-based navigation, personalized dashboards — require more advanced UX thinking and design time. 4. Prototyping and User Testing High-quality app designers often provide interactive prototypes and conduct usability testing. This adds cost but dramatically improves the end product. 5. Designer Experience A seasoned app designer with a strong portfolio of successful apps will command higher rates — but typically delivers better, more refined results.

What's Usually Included in App Design Services?

When you hire a professional app designer, a comprehensive package typically includes:

  • User research and competitive analysis
  • Information architecture (IA) and wireframes
  • High-fidelity UI screens
  • Interactive prototype
  • Style guide and design system
  • Developer handoff files

The Risk of Going Too Cheap

Cutting corners on app design is a costly mistake. Poor UX leads to high drop-off rates, negative reviews, and ultimately, an app that fails to achieve its purpose. Investing in quality design upfront saves you from expensive redesigns later.
